metadata.title: States PCS: Uttarakhand GK for UKPSC Exams

questions.question: In which Uttarakhand city is the cement factory located?
questions.explanation: The cement factory is located in Rani Pur, an industrial area in Haridwar district, Uttarakhand. Rani Pur hosts several large-scale industries and is part of the industrial belt in the region. Other options like Ranikhet and Rani Pokhari are hill towns and do not have major industrial setups. The factory benefits from proximity to transport and raw materials in Rani Pur.

questions.question: Which city in Uttarakhand houses the Central Building Research Institute?
questions.explanation: The Central Building Research Institute (CBRI) was established in 1947 in Roorkee, Uttarakhand. It is a premier CSIR research institute specializing in building science and technology. The Roorkee campus contains advanced labs for research on building materials, construction technologies, and disaster-resistant structures. CBRI supports national and international projects focused on sustainable and affordable housing innovations.

questions.question: In which Uttarakhand city is the Disaster Mitigation and Management Centre located?
questions.explanation: The Disaster Mitigation and Management Centre was established in Dehradun, Uttarakhand, in 2006. It operates under the Department of Disaster Management, Government of Uttarakhand. It is located near the Uttarakhand secretariat compound on Rajpur Road. The centre serves as the state’s apex institute for disaster mitigation and management.

questions.question: In which Uttarakhand city is AIIMS located?
questions.explanation: AIIMS Rishikesh was established in 2012 under PMSSY. It is located on 100 acres along the Ganges in the Himalayas’ foothills in Rishikesh, Uttarakhand. It functions as an autonomous medical institute of national importance.

questions.question: Who is known as the scholar of Dhol Sagar?
questions.explanation: Dhol Sagar is a traditional drum-beat manual of Uttarakhand’s folk culture. Uttam Das is recognized as the authoritative scholar of Dhol Sagar, preserving its rhythmic sequences used in Garhwal and Kumaon rituals. The knowledge of this oral–written tradition is rare, and Uttam Das is considered the only expert in the state associated with it.

questions.question: Pushpesh Pant is known for expertise in which field?
questions.explanation: Pushpesh Pant (born 1946) is an Indian academic and historian. He retired as Professor of International Relations at Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi. He is widely recognized as one of India’s leading experts in international relations and affairs.

questions.question: By what name was Vishweshwar Dutt Saklani known?
questions.explanation: Vishweshwar Dutt Saklani was a freedom fighter and conservationist from Tehri Garhwal, Uttarakhand. He was called “Tree Man” for planting over 50 lakh trees, transforming barren lands into forests. Saklani’s work covered more than 1,200 hectares, and his efforts were recognized by Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi. The planted trees were valued at about 4,500 crore rupees.

questions.question: Who is known as the “Encyclopaedia of Uttarakhand”?
questions.explanation: Shiv Prasad Dabral, also known as “Charan,” authored an 18-volume work titled Uttarakhand ka Itihaas. He extensively researched the region’s history, geography, archaeology, and culture. His writings serve as authoritative references on Uttarakhand’s heritage for scholars and students.

questions.question: Where is the Garhwal Rifles headquarters located since 1887?
questions.explanation: The Garhwal Rifles Regimental Centre has been located at Lansdowne, Pauri Garhwal, Uttarakhand, since 1887. It was established the same year as the 1st Battalion moved to Kaludanda, later renamed Lansdowne after the Viceroy of India. Lansdowne serves as the training centre and headquarters for the regiment.

questions.question: Which Uttarakhand woman received the Black Belt First Dan title from France?
questions.explanation: Kamla Rawat is an Indian judoka from Uttarakhand awarded the Black Belt First Dan by France. The First Dan rank in judo indicates a high level of expertise. This title was conferred in recognition of her performance in national and international judo competitions representing India.
